description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by three prominent metal hoops. The wood appears weathered and dark, suggesting age and exposure to the elements, while the metal bands are slightly tarnished, adding to the rustic appearance. The top of the barrel is flat and closed, with no visible openings or spouts, indicating it is currently sealed. The surface of the wood displays a natural grain pattern, and the metal hoops are riveted for reinforcement. There are no visible handles or decorative markings on the exterior. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban environments such as alleys, storage yards, or near workshops.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with metal hoops and a flat, closed top, showing rustic and utilitarian features. The surface is dark, aged wood with visible grain and tarnished metal bands.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full cylindrical body, three metal hoops, and the flat, closed wooden top.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view revealing the curvature of the staves and the continuous metal banding.
description_view_2: Another angled view emphasizing the seamless construction of the wood and the even spacing of the metal hoops.
description_view_3: Rear view similar to the front, with consistent wood texture and metal reinforcement visible.
